_meta.description: 22-register taxonomy organized into 5 families. Each register describes a distinct writing mode with characteristic linguistic markers and typical use contexts.
_meta.usage: Registers are blended by the synthesizer to produce a personalized style configuration. A user typically has 1 primary, 1-2 secondary, and 0-2 accent registers.

registers.name: Academic-Analytical
registers.description: Evidence-based writing with hedged claims, citations, and formal structure. Prioritizes rigor and precision over readability.
registers.example_context: Journal articles, literature reviews, thesis chapters, conference papers

registers.name: Pedagogical
registers.description: Teaching-oriented writing that scaffolds complexity, leads with examples, and checks understanding. Adjusts density to audience level.
registers.example_context: Textbooks, tutorials, course materials, explainer articles, workshop handouts
linguistic_markers: definition patterns (X is... / By X, we mean...)
comprehension checks (does this make sense?, to recap)

registers.name: Technical-Referential
registers.description: Precise, terminology-dense writing that assumes domain knowledge. Procedural and efficient, minimal personality.
registers.example_context: API documentation, technical specifications, engineering reports, reference manuals

registers.name: Popularizing-Explainer
registers.description: Makes complex topics accessible through analogies, narrative hooks, and controlled jargon introduction. Bridges expert knowledge to general audiences.
registers.example_context: Science journalism, popular books, TED-style talks, explainer videos, blog posts for general audiences
linguistic_markers: imagine if... / think of it like...

registers.name: Corporate-Institutional
registers.description: Polished, risk-averse writing oriented toward stakeholders. Heavy hedging, passive constructions, and carefully managed tone.
registers.example_context: Press releases, annual reports, internal memos, corporate blog posts, stakeholder communications
linguistic_markers: diplomatic hedging (at this time, going forward)

registers.name: Consultative-Advisory
registers.description: Diagnostic, recommendation-oriented writing that presents options with trade-offs. Structured around problems and solutions.
registers.example_context: Consulting reports, strategy documents, advisory memos, proposal responses, recommendation letters
linguistic_markers: option A vs option B structures

registers.name: Journalistic-Reportorial
registers.description: Lead-first, factual writing that attributes sources and maintains neutral stance. Inverted pyramid structure.
registers.example_context: News articles, feature stories, investigative reports, press coverage, magazine articles

registers.name: Legal-Regulatory
registers.description: Precise, exhaustive writing with defined terms and conditional structures. Leaves no room for ambiguity.
registers.example_context: Contracts, terms of service, regulatory filings, compliance documents, policy documents

registers.name: Literary-Poetic
registers.description: Rhythm-aware writing that prioritizes aesthetic quality. Figurative language, compression, and sound-sense interplay.
registers.example_context: Poetry, literary fiction, lyric essays, experimental prose, artist statements

registers.name: Narrative-Storytelling
registers.description: Arc-driven writing with character, scene, and temporal flow. Uses suspense, reveal, and pacing to sustain engagement.
registers.example_context: Narrative nonfiction, case studies told as stories, memoir, feature articles, content marketing
linguistic_markers: temporal markers (one morning, three years later)

registers.name: Design-Critical
registers.description: Aesthetic vocabulary with spatial and material awareness. Positions work within cultural and historical context.
registers.example_context: Art criticism, design reviews, exhibition catalogs, architecture writing, curatorial essays

registers.name: Lyrical-Reflective
registers.description: Meditative, first-person writing with sensory language and associative structure. Personal and exploratory.
registers.example_context: Personal essays, memoir passages, reflective journals, artist notebooks, creative nonfiction
linguistic_markers: I remember / I notice / I wonder

registers.name: Advocacy-Persuasive
registers.description: Call-to-action writing that combines emotional appeal with evidence. Values-driven, urgent, and outcome-oriented.
registers.example_context: Grant proposals, op-eds, campaign materials, fundraising letters, manifestos

registers.name: Facilitative-Workshop
registers.description: Invitational writing that poses questions, holds space for multiple perspectives, and drives toward action.
registers.example_context: Workshop descriptions, facilitation guides, discussion prompts, collaborative design briefs

registers.name: Conversational-Collegial
registers.description: Warm, peer-level writing with contractions, rhetorical questions, and inclusive language. Approachable but substantive.
registers.example_context: Team communications, peer reviews, blog posts, informal talks, Slack messages

registers.name: Mentoring-Coaching
registers.description: Supportive but challenging writing that uses Socratic questioning, celebrates progress, and reframes setbacks constructively.
registers.example_context: Feedback letters, mentoring notes, coaching frameworks, developmental reviews, teaching comments
linguistic_markers: what do you think would happen if...
I notice that you... / have you considered...

registers.name: Social-Thread
registers.description: Fragmentary, hook-driven writing that builds across posts. Conversational pacing optimized for scroll and engagement.
registers.example_context: Twitter/X threads, LinkedIn posts, Mastodon threads, Bluesky posts
linguistic_markers: hook opening (hot take: / unpopular opinion: / thread:)

registers.name: Caption-Micro
registers.description: Ultra-compressed writing paired with visuals. Punchy, hashtag-aware, and optimized for thumbnail attention spans.
registers.example_context: Instagram captions, image descriptions, exhibition labels, poster text, social media cards

registers.name: Newsletter-Epistolary
registers.description: Direct address writing that builds relationships over time. Personal but curated, balancing intimacy with professionalism.
registers.example_context: Email newsletters, Substack posts, membership updates, digest formats

registers.name: Oral-Presentational
registers.description: Written-for-speaking text with shorter sentences, heavy signposting, and strategic repetition for oral delivery.
registers.example_context: Conference talks, keynotes, panel remarks, lecture notes, podcast scripts

registers.name: Documentation-Instructional
registers.description: Step-by-step writing in imperative mood. Scannable, task-oriented, and optimized for people who need to do something now.
registers.example_context: How-to guides, setup instructions, runbooks, onboarding docs, README files

registers.name: Interdisciplinary-Bridge
registers.description: Translates concepts between domains using explicit analogies and dual-vocabulary definitions. Assumes readers know one field but not the other.
registers.example_context: Interdisciplinary papers, cross-field blog posts, design-meets-engineering writing, art-science essays
linguistic_markers: in X terms, this is like... / what Y calls Z
bridging phrases (similarly, the same principle applies)
explicit framing of which audience is being addressed
footnotes or asides for domain-specific depth
structural analogy (just as X does Y, so Z does W)
